q01.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q01.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.

q02.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q02.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.

q03.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q03.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.

q04.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q04.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.

q05.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q05.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.

q06.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q06.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.

q07.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q07.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.

q08.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q08.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.

q09.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q09.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.

q10.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q10.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q11.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q11.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.

q12.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q12.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q13.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q13.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q14.name: The Grudge War
q14.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.

q15.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q15.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.

q16.name: The Flooded Hold
q16.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.

q17.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q17.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.

q18.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q18.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.

q19.name: Wolves at the Folds
q19.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.

q20.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q20.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.

q21.name: Rival Warband
q21.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.

q22.name: The Restless Crypt
q22.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.

q23.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q23.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q24.name: The Restless Crypt
q24.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.

q25.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q25.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.

q26.name: The Proving Hunt
q26.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.

q27.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q27.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.

q28.name: Rival Warband
q28.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.

q29.name: Webs in the Canopy
q29.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.

q30.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q30.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.

q31.name: The Blighted Glade
q31.desc: Something soured the heartwood; what lives there now bites. The wardens want it cut out.

q32.name: Webs in the Canopy
